mnemonics.title: PIE Signing Pattern
mnemonics.content: Odd terms ADD (+), Even terms SUBTRACT (-). Like ODD=ADD, EVEN=SUBTRACT: O-A-E-S

acronyms.usage: Used for counting union of sets with overlaps
Count permutations with no fixed points: Dₙ = n! × Σ(-1)ᵏ/k!
Count of integers ≤ n coprime to n, computed using PIE: φ(n) = nΠ(1-1/p)
Number of ways to partition m objects into n nonempty subsets

visualTips.visualization: Draw two overlapping circles. Label intersection area as the overlap that gets double-counted and must be subtracted once.
Three circles in a triangle formation. Center triangle (triple intersection) gets counted 3 times, subtracted 3 times in pairs, so must be added back once.
Think of + - + - pattern: singles +, pairs -, triples +, quads -. Each level alternates the operation.
Remember: Add singles (undercount), subtract pairs (overcount), add triples (undercount again). The pattern corrects the count.

analogies.analogy: If people attend multiple parties, add attendance at each party, subtract those counted twice, add back those counted three times - like a headcount with duplicate detection.
Painting three overlapping rectangular strips. Apply paint in order: first strip (blue), second (red) - overlaps get darker, third (green) - some triple overlaps. Final color represents final count.
Tax collectors from different departments might tax the same person. Each department adds their list, overlaps get taxed twice so subtract, but triple-taxed must be added back.
